title: link
text: lnk_barrier_overrides(conn, barriers, observations, habitat, exclusions, control, params, ) — processes fish observations habitat confirmations barrier skip list fresh. Counts observations upstream barrier via fwa_upstream() SQL, applies per-species thresholds, unions habitat confirmations. Control table (barriers_definite_control barrier_ind = TRUE rows) blocks override flagged positions — gated per-species params$observation_control_apply residents (BT, WCT) can still override anadromous-blocking falls. Habitat path bypasses control entirely (expert-confirmed habitat higher-trust observations). Output: (blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, species_code) table fresh skips access gating.

title: link
text: Six-phase bcfishpass-reproducing pipeline, driven lnk_config(): - lnk_pipeline_setup(conn, schema, overwrite) — create per-run working schema. - lnk_pipeline_load(conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — crossings + modelled fixes + PSCIS status overrides. - lnk_pipeline_prepare(conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — falls, definite + control, habitat confirms, gradient barriers, natural_barriers, barrier overrides, per-model minimal reduction, base segments. Uses manifest-key gating cfg$overrides$barriers_definite_control cfg$habitat_classification (DB probes). - lnk_pipeline_break(conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — sequential frs_break_apply config-defined order: observations → gradient minimal → barriers_definite (separate break source) → habitat endpoints → crossings. - lnk_pipeline_classify(conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — assembles fresh.streams_breaks (gradient FULL + falls + barriers_definite + crossings, WSG-filtered) runs frs_habitat_classify(). barriers_definite enters directly bcfishpass appends user-definite post-filter (via observation override). - lnk_pipeline_connect(conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — per-species cluster + connected_waterbody. - lnk_pipeline_species(cfg, aoi) — canonical helper “species config classifies AOI” (intersects cfg$parameters_fresh cfg$wsg_species presence).

title: link
text: lnk_source(conn, crossings, label_col, label_map) — returns list(table, label_col, label_map) plugs directly frs_habitat(break_sources = list(...)). label_map translates link severity → fresh access labels (high → blocked, moderate → potential). lnk_aggregate(conn, crossings, habitat, cols_sum) — per-crossing upstream habitat rollup fresh output. Sums spawning_km, rearing_km (custom metrics).

title: link
text: data flows directions: link → fresh (scored crossings break sources) fresh → link (habitat classification upstream rollup).
code: # link scores crossings lnk_load(conn, csv = "overrides.csv", to = "working.fixes") lnk_override(conn, "working.crossings", "working.fixes") lnk_score(conn, "working.crossings")  # link produces break source spec src <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings")  # fresh consumes it — zero translation frs_habitat(conn, "MORR", break_sources = list(src))  # link reads fresh output for per-crossing rollup lnk_aggregate(conn, "working.crossings", "fresh.streams_habitat")

text: changing configs/<name>/dimensions.csv file feeds lnk_rules_build(): Regenerate + diff rules.yaml running anything. Rscript data-raw/build_rules.R git diff inst/extdata/configs/<name>/rules.yaml. Confirm diff matches intent — e.g., toggling spawn_lake=SK remove waterbody_type: L rule SK.spawn. Catching unintended rule costs seconds; catching 20-min tar_make costs 20 min. Pre-flight one WSG, five. reinstalling package (pak::local_install(upgrade = FALSE, ask = FALSE) equivalent), run single-WSG workload (link-tarmake-single <WSG>) smallest WSG impacted change full link-tarmake-5wsg. ADMS smallest, BULK largest per workloads.csv — pick whichever small exercises affected species. Always report pre-flight result framed departures bcfishpass reference, just raw link numbers. Small config changes (new threshold, edge-type tweak) land within ~±20% bcfp values; >50% departure flag; >100% 10× swing “investigate rerun” — bcfishpass mature numbers reasonable sanity baseline. four sources buckets §6 research doc better signal scalar rollup delta — run pre-flight WSG . DB-side sanity shortcut. fix’s effect measurable (e.g., “SK spawning km drop lake shoreline edges longer count”), direct query fresh.streams_habitat grouped edge_type can confirm direction change ~30s without tar_make.

title: link
text: Issues, PR descriptions, commit messages client-visible deliverables, internal notes. Avoid artifacts: - Framing work unsolicited unpaid (“assigned client”) - Self-justifying adjectives (“defensible”, “rigorous”) — show, don’t claim - Internal workflow meta (PWF refs, SRED xrefs, planning context) - Performative effort language (“attempts unsuccessful”) — state factual current state Integrity-preserving ≠ self-effacing. Factual, performatively humble. Scope: repo artifacts (issues, PRs, commits, reports). apply internal planning docs, CLAUDE.md, chat. Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

what: GitHub Issue Creation - Always Use Files
title: link
text: gh issue create command heredoc syntax fails repeatedly EOF errors. ALWAYS use --body-file:
code: cat > /tmp/issue_body.md << 'EOF' ## Problem ...  ## Proposed Solution ... EOF  gh issue create --title "Brief technical title" --body-file /tmp/issue_body.md

title: link
text: : Close issues via commit messages. commit closure documentation. DON’T: Close issues gh issue close. breaks audit trail — ’s linked diff showing changed. Fixes #N Closes #N — auto-closes links commit issue Relates #N — partial progress, close Always close issues work complete. Don’t leave stale open issues.

MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: Crossing Connectivity Interpretation
text: Load validate — read correction CSVs database provenance tracking referential integrity checks Match — link features different sources (PSCIS assessments, modelled crossings, culvert inventories) using network position bidirectional deduplication Override — apply hand-reviewed corrections accumulated across field seasons, flag orphans duplicates Score — classify features biological impact (severity) weighted multi-criteria ranking (prioritization) Interpret barriers — process observations habitat confirmations per-species barrier skip lists using fwa_upstream() network topology Bridge fresh — produce break source specs barrier override tables plug directly frs_habitat()

title: Crossing Connectivity Interpretation
code: library(link)  conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Load crossings, apply corrections lnk_load(conn, csv = "overrides.csv", to = "working.fixes") lnk_override(conn, "working.crossings", "working.fixes")  # Score severity lnk_score(conn, "working.crossings", method = "severity")  # Build barrier override list from observations + habitat confirmations lnk_barrier_overrides(conn,   barriers = "working.natural_barriers",   observations = "bcfishobs.observations",   habitat = "working.user_habitat_classification",   params = params_fresh,   to = "working.barrier_overrides")  # Bridge to fresh src <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings") fresh::frs_habitat(conn, "MORR",   break_sources = list(src),   barrier_overrides = "working.barrier_overrides")  # Per-crossing upstream habitat rollup lnk_aggregate(conn, "working.crossings", "fresh.streams_habitat")

title: Crossing Connectivity Interpretation
text: primitives assemble six-phase pipeline classifies spawning + rearing habitat per segment watershed group. config bundle declares manifest rules, parameters, override CSVs drive run; bundled "bcfishpass" config expresses bcfishpass’s classification slice (spawning + rearing predicates, natural-barrier modelling, observation-based access overrides) self-contained input. data-raw/_targets.R wraps five validated WSGs (ADMS, BULK, BABL, ELKR, DEAD) targets caching cross-WSG regression. vignette walking bundled bcfishpass config preparation; meanwhile per-phase detail lives research/bcfishpass_comparison.md.
code: cfg <- lnk_config("bcfishpass")  lnk_pipeline_setup(conn, schema = "working_bulk", overwrite = TRUE) lnk_pipeline_load(conn,    aoi = "BULK", cfg = cfg, schema = "working_bulk") lnk_pipeline_prepare(conn, aoi = "BULK", cfg = cfg, schema = "working_bulk") lnk_pipeline_break(conn,   aoi = "BULK", cfg = cfg, schema = "working_bulk") lnk_pipeline_classify(conn, aoi = "BULK", cfg = cfg, schema = "working_bulk") lnk_pipeline_connect(conn, aoi = "BULK", cfg = cfg, schema = "working_bulk")

title: Compute upstream habitat per crossing — lnk_aggregate
text: half prioritization: severity alone tell invest. high-severity barrier 50m upstream habitat lower priority moderate barrier 15km spawning habitat. Flexible aggregation: cols_sum lets sum habitat metric — just spawning/rearing. Lake rearing hectares, wetland area, total accessible length — whatever habitat classification produced. Data flow:   link scores crossings. fresh segments network classifies habitat. link reads fresh's output compute per-crossing rollups.
code: link (score crossings) -> fresh (segment, classify) -> link (rollup)

title: Compute upstream habitat per crossing — lnk_aggregate
code: # --- "Two barriers are both high severity. #      One blocks 0.3km of rearing habitat. #      The other blocks 12km of spawning habitat for chinook. #      Which do you fix first?" --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Score crossings lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Compute upstream habitat from fresh output lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat") # Added spawning_km, rearing_km to working.crossings # Summary: #   spawning_km: min=0.0, median=2.3, max=45.1 #   rearing_km:  min=0.0, median=5.7, max=89.2  # Now you have severity + habitat — the full picture # ORDER BY severity DESC, spawning_km DESC # to find high-severity barriers blocking the most habitat.  # --- Custom metrics --- lnk_aggregate(conn,   crossings = "working.crossings",   habitat = "fresh.streams_habitat",   cols_sum = c(spawning_km = "spawning",                rearing_km = "rearing",                lake_ha = "lake_rearing")) } # }

code: lnk_barrier_overrides(   conn,   barriers,   observations = NULL,   habitat = NULL,   exclusions = NULL,   control = NULL,   params,   cols_index = c("blue_line_key", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree"),   to,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build barrier override list from evidence sources — lnk_barrier_overrides
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. barriers Character. Schema-qualified table barriers columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode_ltree, localcode_ltree, label. Typically fresh.streams_breaks. observations Character NULL. Schema-qualified table fish observations columns: species_code, blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, wscode, localcode, observation_date. Typically bcfishobs.observations. habitat Character NULL. Schema-qualified table confirmed habitat columns: species_code, blue_line_key, upstream_route_measure, habitat_ind. confirmed habitat upstream barrier removes (threshold = 1). exclusions Character NULL. Schema-qualified table observation exclusions column fish_observation_point_id. Flagged observations removed counting. control Character NULL. Schema-qualified table barrier controls columns: blue_line_key, downstream_route_measure, barrier_ind. Barriers table barrier_ind = TRUE overridden observations — species params$observation_control_apply TRUE. Resident species routinely inhabit reaches upstream anadromous-blocking falls (post-glacial connectivity, ocean-return requirement), observations still count unless flag says otherwise. Habitat confirmations (habitat argument) higher-trust observations — bypass control table entirely, species. params Data frame per-species parameters. Must columns: species_code, observation_threshold, observation_date_min, observation_buffer_m, observation_species. Optional column observation_control_apply (logical) — TRUE, control table blocks overrides species; FALSE/NA/missing, species ignores control. Bcfishpass defaults: TRUE CH/CM/CO/PK/SK/ST, FALSE BT/WCT. See configs/bcfishpass/parameters_fresh.csv. cols_index Character vector. Column names index barriers table fwa_upstream() performance. Indexes created EXISTS. Default c("blue_line_key", "wscode_ltree", "localcode_ltree") — columns exist table indexed. Character. Schema-qualified output table name. verbose Logical. Report counts.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums — lnk_config_verify
text: Recomputes sha256 every file declared bundle's provenance: block compares recorded checksum. Returns tibble expected vs observed; flags drift.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums — lnk_config_verify
code: lnk_config_verify(cfg, strict = FALSE)

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums — lnk_config_verify
text: cfg lnk_config object lnk_config(). strict Logical. TRUE, errors file drifted. Default FALSE warns returns tibble inspection.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums — lnk_config_verify
text: tibble columns: file — path relative cfg$dir expected — checksum recorded manifest (sha256 hex) observed — checksum recomputed current file (sha256 hex) drift — logical, TRUE expected != observed missing — logical, TRUE file longer exists disk (observed NA case) tibble carries one row per provenanced file. bundle provenance: block (cfg$provenance NULL) returns empty tibble columns.

title: Verify Config Bundle File Checksums — lnk_config_verify
text: Use run time detect silent drift — file edited without re-recording checksum, external CSV re-synced path. Drift two pipeline runs DB state package versions almost always traces back config-file edit; lnk_config_verify() fastest way localize change.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. csv Character. Path CSV file, vector paths load multiple files table (e.g., one per field season). Character. Schema-qualified destination table name (e.g., "working.overrides_modelled"). cols_id Character. Column(s) used crossing identifier. System-agnostic — "stream_crossing_id", "chris_culvert_id", ID system uses. cols_required Character vector. Columns must exist every CSV. Fails fast informative error naming missing column file. cols_id always required need repeated . cols_provenance Character vector. Provenance columns track reviewed . Kept present CSV, silently skipped absent. Set NULL disable provenance tracking. overwrite Logical. TRUE (default), drop recreate table. FALSE, append existing table.

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: destination table name (invisibly), piping lnk_override_validate() lnk_override_apply().

title: Load override CSVs into a database table — lnk_load
text: Override CSVs represent hand-reviewed crossing corrections accumulated across field seasons imagery review. row says "crossing's attribute changed value." Fail fast: structure validated data written. Missing required columns produce clear error naming column file path. Multi-file load: pass vector CSV paths combine overrides different sources (field seasons, watersheds, reviewers). first file creates table; subsequent files append. Provenance optional: teams mature QA processes track reviewer, review_date, source. New projects can start without columns add later.

Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review #> 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 4                 1004            PASSABLE A. Irvine  2025-09-20    field visit #> 5                 1005             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-10-01 imagery review #   modelled_crossing_id barrier_result_code  reviewer review_date        source # 1                 1001            PASSABLE  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 2                 1002             BARRIER  J. Smith  2025-08-15 imagery review # 3                 1003                NONE A. Irvine  2025-09-20   field visit # ... # Each row corrects one crossing. The reviewer and date tell you # who made the call and when — your audit trail.  # --- Load into database (the typical workflow) --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Single file — most common case lnk_load(conn,   csv  = "data/overrides/modelled_xings_fixes.csv",   to   = "working.overrides_modelled",   cols_required = c("barrier_result_code"))  # Multiple files from different field seasons lnk_load(conn,   csv  = c("data/overrides/2024_field.csv",            "data/overrides/2025_field.csv"),   to   = "working.overrides_modelled")  # Then validate and apply: lnk_override_validate(conn, "working.overrides_modelled",   "working.crossings") lnk_override_apply(conn, "working.crossings",   "working.overrides_modelled") } # }

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: conn DBI::DBIConnection object. sources List source specs. spec named list : table (required) Schema-qualified table name. col_id (required) ID column source. (optional) Raw SQL filter. Developer API — applied within subquery. col_blk (optional) Override network key column. col_measure (optional) Override measure column. xref_csv Character. Optional path CSV known matches. Must two columns matching col_id first two sources. Applied first — matched IDs excluded spatial matching. col_blk Character. Default network key column. col_measure Character. Default measure column. distance Numeric. Maximum network distance (metres) match. Character. Output table name. overwrite Logical. Overwrite output table exists. verbose Logical. Report match counts.

title: Match crossing records across data systems — lnk_match
text: N-way matching: two sources produce pairwise comparisons. 1:1 dedup: two-pass DISTINCT keeps closest match per record sides. many--many inflation. xref priority: xref_csv provided, known matches applied first (distance = 0). Already-matched IDs excluded spatial matching.

title: Produce a fresh-compatible break source list — lnk_source
code: # --- The link -> fresh handoff --- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn()  # Step 1: Score crossings with link lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings")  # Step 2: Produce break source src <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings") # Returns: # list(table = "working.crossings", #      label_col = "severity", #      label_map = c(high = "blocked", moderate = "potential"))  # Step 3: Feed to fresh — link's output is fresh's input frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(src))  # --- Combine with other break sources --- frs_habitat(conn, "BULK", break_sources = list(   src,   list(table = "working.falls", label = "blocked"),   list(table = "working.dams", label = "blocked"))) # link scored crossings + falls + dams — all as break sources.  # --- Custom label_map for a conservative project --- # Only treat high-severity as blocked src_strict <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label_map = c(high = "blocked"))  # --- Static label for all crossings --- src_all <- lnk_source(conn, "working.crossings",   label = "potential", label_col = NULL) # Every crossing is a potential barrier — no severity differentiation. } # }

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: csv Path CSV file columns severity, metric, value. provided, CSV values override -code defaults. Use system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") see expected format. high Named list metric thresholds high severity. title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: named list elements high, moderate, low, containing named list metric thresholds (numeric values keyed metric name).

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
text: Thresholds control lnk_score_severity() classifies crossings. default values reflect BC provincial fish passage assessment criteria: High severity outlet_drop >= 0.6m slope_length >= 120 — impassable species flows Moderate severity outlet_drop >= 0.3m slope_length >= 60 — flow-dependent, potentially passable migration flows Low severity everything else crossing present System-agnostic: metric names user-defined strings, hardcoded provincial data system. New Zealand user might define high = list(perch_height = 0.5, pipe_gradient = 0.05). Merge order: code defaults < CSV values < inline arguments. lets ship project CSV still tweak one threshold inline.

title: Load configurable severity scoring thresholds — lnk_thresholds
code: # Default BC thresholds — zero config th <- lnk_thresholds() th$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.6 # [1] 0.6  # Override from bundled CSV (same result, shows the format) csv_path <- system.file("extdata", "thresholds_default.csv", package = "link") th_csv <- lnk_thresholds(csv = csv_path) identical(th, th_csv) #> [1] TRUE  # Project-specific: bull trout tolerate higher drops th_bt <- lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8)) th_bt$high$outlet_drop #> [1] 0.8 # [1] 0.8 — inline override wins  # How thresholds plug into scoring (the integration point) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- lnk_db_conn() lnk_score_severity(conn, "working.crossings",   thresholds = lnk_thresholds(high = list(outlet_drop = 0.8))) } # }

New lnk_config_verify(cfg, strict) recomputes sha256 every provenanced file returns tibble (file, expected, observed, drift, missing). Default warns drift; strict = TRUE errors. digest added Suggests. Tests: 93 new — provenance parsing, drift detection (clean / mutated / missing / strict), bundled-config drift = 0 invariants, stamp shape + markdown rendering + finalization + db-snapshot opt-.

Add data-raw/_targets.R — tar_map(wsg = c("ADMS", "BULK", "BABL", "ELKR")) per-AOI target function, synchronous execution, dplyr::bind_rows rollup. fresh.streams shared schema single-host parallelism collide — runs serially today; distributed runs (M4 + M1) follow-alongside fresh upstream change per-AOI output paths (#38) Add data-raw/compare_bcfishpass_wsg(wsg, config) — per-AOI target function. Wraps six lnk_pipeline_* phases, diffs output bcfishpass.habitat_linear_* reference tunnel DB, returns ~10-row tibble (wsg × species × habitat_type × link_km × bcfishpass_km × diff_pct). KB-scale — safe ship SSH. Promote .lnk_pipeline_classify_species exported lnk_pipeline_species(cfg, aoi) — canonical public API “species config classifies AOI.” Used lnk_pipeline_classify lnk_pipeline_connect internally targets per-AOI function externally. Removes duplicate private helper briefly inlined data-raw/. End--end verification (data-raw/logs/20260422_11_tar_make_final.txt) — 4 WSGs / 34 rows produced 8.5 minutes wall clock (serial). Reproducibility: consecutive tar_make() invocations DB state produce bit-identical rollup tibbles. Parity bcfishpass (informational): 34 diff_pct values within 5% reference; research-doc drift (BT rearing: -0.7 → -1.1 pp) traces env state 2026-04-15 today, pipeline non-determinism.

title: link 0.3.0
text: Pipeline phase helpers extract bcfishpass comparison orchestration composable building blocks. 635-line data-raw/compare_bcfishpass.R now 136 lines sequenced helper calls. Add lnk_pipeline_setup() — create per-run working schema (#38) Add lnk_pipeline_load() — load crossings apply modelled-fix PSCIS overrides Add lnk_pipeline_prepare() — load falls / definite / control / habitat CSVs, detect gradient barriers, compute per-species barrier skip list, reduce minimal set via fresh::frs_barriers_minimal(), load base segments Add lnk_pipeline_break() — sequential frs_break_apply observations / gradient / definite / habitat / crossings config-defined order Add lnk_pipeline_classify() — assemble access-gating breaks table run fresh::frs_habitat_classify() Add lnk_pipeline_connect() — per-species rearing-spawning clustering connected-waterbody rules Canonical signature (conn, aoi, cfg, schema) — aoi follows fresh convention (WSG code today; extends ltree / sf polygons / mapsheets later), schema caller’s per-run namespace (working_<aoi> convention) parallel runs collide cfg$species parsed rules YAML lnk_config() load — intersects cfg$wsg_species presence pick per-AOI classify targets Requires fresh 0.14.0 (frs_barriers_minimal)
